title: Best AI Lead Qualifiers for Real Estate in 2026
description: We evaluated Structurely, Lofty AI Assistant, and Real Geeks Robin AI to find which lead qualifier fits your team size and budget.
tldr: Structurely offers transparent usage-based pricing starting at $499/mo with $0.08 per action credit, ideal for teams of 2–10 who want pay-as-you-go AI calling, texting, and live transfers. Lofty AI Assistant and Real Geeks Robin AI lack public pricing; contact vendors directly. If you need predictable costs and visible ROI metrics, Structurely is the clearest option today.

body.text: Lead follow-up is the bottleneck most agents never admit exists. Inbound inquiries sit in the CRM for hours or days, cooling from hot to warm to dead. AI lead qualifiers promise to engage every lead instantly across call, text, and email—then transfer the ready ones to you. We looked at Structurely, Lofty AI Assistant, and Real Geeks Robin AI to see which tools deliver transparent pricing, clear workflows, and measurable ROI for working agents.

We reviewed vendor pricing pages, feature disclosures, and public billing models. We did not fabricate testing scenarios or invent call volumes. Where vendors did not publish pricing or feature details, we note that gap explicitly. The goal: show you which AI qualifiers give you cost certainty, which lock you into sales calls, and which tradeoffs matter for solo agents versus teams.

Structurely charges usage-based action credits: $0.08 per credit on the Team plan ($499/mo platform fee), $0.06 on Company ($999/mo). One credit equals one SMS, 10 seconds of AI talk time, or two emails. No cap on lead volume; you pay only when the AI works. Four pre-built agents are configured for you, with unlimited self-built agents via the agent builder. Multi-agent workflows, live transfer optimization, and local area code numbers are standard. Onboarding is $2,000 (Team) or $2,500 (Company) one-time. Annual contracts are default; month-to-month adds 20%. The vendor claims 3–21x average ROI and offers a pilot period to prove unit economics before long-term commitment.

body.name: Structurely
body.price: Team plan $499/mo + $0.08/action credit; Company plan $999/mo + $0.06/action credit; one-time onboarding $2,000–$2,500
pros: Usage-based billing—no wasted credits or idle charges
Transparent action-credit math (1 SMS = 1 credit, 10s talk = 1 credit)
Live transfer optimization and multi-agent workflows included
60-day dedicated account manager on all paid plans
No lead volume cap; scales with actual activity
cons: Action credits add unpredictable variable cost if lead volume spikes
Team plan limited to 4 pre-built agents; scaling requires self-build or upgrade

body.text: No public pricing or feature page was available for Lofty AI Assistant. Contact the vendor directly for current pricing, feature set, and integration details.

body.name: Lofty AI Assistant
pros: Part of the broader Lofty CRM ecosystem (if you already use Lofty)
cons: No public pricing or feature transparency
Likely bundled with Lofty CRM; unclear if available standalone
Cannot evaluate ROI or cost predictability without vendor call

body.text: No public pricing or feature page was available for Real Geeks Robin AI. Contact the vendor directly for current pricing, feature set, and integration details.

body.name: Real Geeks Robin AI
pros: Native integration with Real Geeks CRM (if you already use Real Geeks)
cons: No public pricing or feature transparency
Likely requires Real Geeks CRM subscription; unclear standalone availability
Cannot evaluate cost or ROI without vendor engagement

items: Teams of 2–10 who want transparent, pay-per-action pricing: Structurely Team plan. You see exactly what you pay for (one SMS = one credit), no idle seat fees, and a 60-day AM to optimize transfers.
Larger brokerages (10+ agents) with high lead volume: Structurely Company plan at $0.06/credit gives volume discounts and custom agent configurations.
Agents already locked into Lofty or Real Geeks CRM: Ask your account rep for bundled AI pricing. Switching CRMs to get AI is expensive; native integration may justify opaque pricing.
Solo agents with unpredictable lead flow: Structurely's no-minimum, no-cap model lets you scale costs with activity. Month-to-month is available if you accept the 20% premium.
Anyone needing cost certainty before commitment: Only Structurely publishes pricing and offers a pilot period to prove ROI with your actual leads.

Lofty AI Assistant and Real Geeks Robin AI until they publish pricing and feature pages. Opaque pricing means you negotiate blind; vendors control the anchor. If you're not already a customer, you're trading time for a sales pitch with no baseline to compare.
Structurely's month-to-month option unless you're piloting or have seasonal lead flow. The 20% premium ($599/mo vs. $499/mo on Team) adds $1,200/year with no additional features.
Any AI qualifier that charges per-seat or per-agent instead of per-action. You pay for idle capacity, not outcomes. Structurely's credit model aligns cost with actual work.
